modules.name: ctlepy.formatted_output
documentation: ctle Copyright (c) 2023 Ulrik Lindahl
Licensed under the MIT license https://github.com/Cooolrik/ctle/blob/main/LICENSE

classes.name: ctlepy.formatted_output.formatted_output

methods.name: ctlepy.formatted_output.formatted_output.blk
documentation: Add an indented block of code

methods.name: ctlepy.formatted_output.formatted_output.tab
documentation: Add an indented block of code, only indent, no braces

methods.name: ctlepy.formatted_output.formatted_output.ln
documentation: Add a single line, at current indentation, (or no indentation if no_indent is True).

methods.name: ctlepy.formatted_output.formatted_output.blk_ln
documentation: Add a single-line block, at the current indentation. Optionally add a comma at the end.
single line block

methods.name: ctlepy.formatted_output.formatted_output.comment_ln
documentation: Add a commend, which is wrapped if longer than the comment_wrap length

methods.name: ctlepy.formatted_output.formatted_output.generate_license_header
documentation: Generates the license header and injects into the generated c++ file

methods.name: ctlepy.formatted_output.formatted_output.inline_file
documentation: Injects the contents of another file, indented at the current indentation level

methods.name: ctlepy.formatted_output.formatted_output.generate_push_and_disable_warnings
documentation: Insert a block of warning disable pragmas for VC and GCC. Pushes all warnings before disabling.

methods.name: ctlepy.formatted_output.formatted_output.generate_pop_warnings
documentation: Insert a block which pops the disabled warnings for VC or GCC

methods.name: ctlepy.formatted_output.formatted_output.write_lines_to_file
documentation: Write the formatted text to a file, but only if the file is not already exactly identical to the formatted text
formatted_output defined a class which can be used to more easily generate formatted output of C/C++ files. The class supports different kinds of indentation, and wraps comments.

modules.name: ctlepy.enum_generator
documentation: ctle Copyright (c) 2023 Ulrik Lindahl
Licensed under the MIT license https://github.com/Cooolrik/ctle/blob/main/LICENSE

functions.name: ctlepy.enum_generator.generate_enum_declaration
documentation: name_padding - minimum number of chars in name, (will be right-padded with spaces if less)
value_padding - minimum number of chars in value, (will be right-padded with spaces if less)
pad_to_max - automatically increase paddings based on names and values in the enum

functions.name: ctlepy.enum_generator.generate_enum_function_implementations
documentation: if use_lowercase_strings is True, all strings will be generated as lowercase.
if use_lowercase_strings is False, the strings will be generated identical as the enum values
